title: Insurance Underwriters
description: Review individual applications for insurance to evaluate degree of risk involved and determine acceptance of applications.

tasks.text: Examine documents to determine degree of risk from factors such as applicant health, financial standing and value, and condition of property.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write to field representatives, medical personnel, or others to obtain further information, quote rates, or explain company underwriting policies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Evaluate possibility of losses due to catastrophe or excessive insurance.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Review company records to determine amount of insurance in force on single risk or group of closely related risks.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Decrease value of policy when risk is substandard and specify applicable endorsements or apply rating to ensure safe, profitable distribution of risks, using reference materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Authorize reinsurance of policy when risk is high.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
